_description: Baseline for CI regression gate. Captured via `make bench-save` on the commit that introduces this file. CI fails if any non-skipped benchmark's ns_per_op or allocs_per_op exceeds (baseline * (1 + tolerance_percent/100)). Regenerate after an intentional perf change with `make bench-save && mv test/bench/latest.json test/bench/baseline.json` and note the delta in CHANGELOG.
_machine: darwin/arm64 Apple M1 Max — CI should capture its own on the runner class it gates against
_tolerance_notes: 50% accommodates non-dedicated-machine jitter on Call-path benchmarks. The gate catches 1.5× regressions — design for 'perf is roughly the same order of magnitude' rather than 'perf is identical to last week'. Tune downward when CI runs on dedicated hardware.
_skip_reason: KitNew is SES-init sensitive (9×10⁸ ns/op); envelope benches are sub-microsecond and jitter ±50% on non-dedicated CI. Gate focuses on BenchmarkCall / BenchmarkCallParallel — the user-visible round-trip path, millisecond scale, where a real regression is obvious.
